title: TC-BUG-001: App Lifecycle Recovery — Device missing after system BT pairing, recovery via background/foreground cycle (no force-kill)
steps: Precondition: Poly device (Focus 2) is powered off and not in Bluetooth memory of phone.
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app. Verify home screen shows empty device list.
2. Background the app (press Home button).
3. Open system Bluetooth settings. Initiate pairing with Focus 2. Complete pairing.
4. Return to Poly Studio Mobile app (foreground). Observe device list.
5. If device appears → PASS (no bug triggered). If device is missing → continue to step 6.
6. Press Home button to background the app again.
7. Wait 3 seconds. Return to app (foreground). Observe device list.
8. If device still missing → force-kill app and relaunch. Verify device appears.
expected: Step 4: Device may be missing (bug signature). Step 7: Device list MUST auto-update and show Focus 2 without force-kill. Actual (If Bug): Device remains missing after background/foreground cycle, requiring force-kill to appear.

title: TC-BUG-002: Device-Specific Asymmetry — Pairing flow on Focus 2, Legend 30, and Voyager 6200
steps: Precondition: Phone forgets all Poly devices. App is installed and logged in.
1. Run this test case 3 times, once per device model: Focus 2, Legend 30, Voyager 6200.
2. Open Poly Studio Mobile app. Verify empty device list.
3. Background app. Pair [Device Model] via system Bluetooth settings.
4. Return to app. Observe device list.
5. If device missing → background app (Home), wait 3s, return to app. Observe.
6. If still missing → force-kill app, relaunch. Observe.
7. Note: For each model, record: (a) Does device appear immediately? (b) Does background/foreground cycle fix it? (c) Is force-kill required?
expected: All three devices (Focus 2, Legend 30, Voyager 6200) MUST exhibit identical behavior: either all appear immediately, or all recover via background/foreground cycle. Actual (If Bug): One or more devices require force-kill while others recover normally, indicating device-specific Bluetooth stack asymmetry.

title: TC-BUG-003: Disconnected-to-Connected State Transition — Device visible in list but not updating status on power-on
steps: Precondition: Focus 2 is already paired and visible in Poly Studio Mobile app device list (disconnected state, greyed out).
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app. Verify Focus 2 is visible in device list with 'Disconnected' status.
2. Power ON Focus 2 headset. Wait 5 seconds.
3. Observe device list in app without navigating away or refreshing.
4. Note: Do NOT background or force-kill the app.
5. If status does NOT update to 'Connected' within 10 seconds → background app (Home), wait 3s, return.
6. Observe if status updates after foreground cycle.
7. If still not connected → force-kill and relaunch. Verify connection status.
expected: Step 3: Device status MUST update from 'Disconnected' to 'Connected' in real-time within 10 seconds without any app lifecycle intervention. Actual (If Bug): Status remains 'Disconnected' indefinitely until app is backgrounded/foregrounded or force-killed.

title: TC-BUG-004: Timing/Race Condition — Device list refresh at varying intervals after system BT pairing
steps: Precondition: Phone forgets all Poly devices. App is installed.
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app. Verify empty device list.
2. Background app. Pair Focus 2 via system Bluetooth settings.
3. Immediately return to app (0s delay). Record: Does device appear? (Y/N)
4. If No → wait 1s in app. Does device appear? (Y/N)
5. If No → wait 5s in app. Does device appear? (Y/N)
6. If No → wait 10s in app. Does device appear? (Y/N)
7. If No → background app, return immediately. Does device appear? (Y/N)
8. If No → force-kill and relaunch. Does device appear? (Y/N)
9. Repeat steps 1-8 for Legend 30 and Voyager 6200.
expected: Device MUST appear in the list within 5 seconds of returning to the app after pairing, without requiring any app lifecycle action. Actual (If Bug): Device appears only after background/foreground cycle or force-kill, with timing varying across attempts (intermittent race condition).

title: TC-BUG-005: Multi-Device List Refresh — Pairing a second device while first device is in disconnected state
steps: Precondition: Focus 2 is already paired and visible in app device list (disconnected).
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app. Verify Focus 2 is visible with 'Disconnected' status.
2. Background app (Home button).
3. Open system Bluetooth settings. Pair Legend 30 as a second device.
4. Return to Poly Studio Mobile app. Observe device list.
5. Verify: (a) Focus 2 still visible? (b) Legend 30 appears in list? (c) Status of each device.
6. If Legend 30 is missing → background app, wait 3s, return to app. Observe.
7. If still missing → force-kill app and relaunch. Verify both devices appear.
8. Tap on Legend 30 to connect. Verify connection succeeds.
expected: Step 5: Both Focus 2 (Disconnected) AND Legend 30 (Disconnected or Connecting) MUST appear in the device list. Adding a second device MUST NOT cause the first device to disappear. Actual (If Bug): Legend 30 is missing from list, or Focus 2 disappears when Legend 30 is paired, requiring app restart to see both devices.

title: TC-BUG-006: Deep Lifecycle — Device missing after system BT pairing survives phone reboot
steps: Precondition: Phone forgets all Poly devices. App is installed.
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app. Verify empty device list.
2. Background app. Pair Focus 2 via system Bluetooth settings.
3. Return to app. If device appears → PASS. If missing → continue.
4. Without force-killing app, reboot the phone.
5. After phone reboots, open Poly Studio Mobile app.
6. Observe device list. Does Focus 2 appear?
7. If missing → background app (Home), wait 3s, return. Observe.
8. If still missing → force-kill app and relaunch. Verify device appears.
expected: Step 6: Focus 2 MUST appear in the device list after phone reboot without any app lifecycle intervention. The pairing state is persisted at the OS level and the app must read it on cold start. Actual (If Bug): Device list remains empty after reboot, requiring background/foreground cycle or force-kill to populate.

title: TC-BUG-007: Concurrent Pairing During Media Playback — Device list refresh with audio streaming active
steps: Precondition: Phone is playing music via speaker or wired headphones.
1. Start media playback (music/podcast) on phone speaker.
2. Open Poly Studio Mobile app. Verify empty device list.
3. Background app. Pair Focus 2 via system Bluetooth settings while media continues playing.
4. Return to Poly Studio Mobile app (media still playing on phone speaker).
5. Observe device list. Does Focus 2 appear?
6. If missing → background app, return. Observe.
7. If still missing → force-kill app, relaunch. Verify device appears.
8. Stop media playback.
expected: Step 5: Focus 2 MUST appear in device list even when media is actively streaming. Audio state MUST NOT interfere with BLE scan or device list refresh. Actual (If Bug): Device list remains empty while media is playing, but populates correctly when media is stopped.
